must_fix: apps/core/src/platform/operator-api.ts:763 and apps/core/src/platform/operator-api.ts:806 must map service-thrown post-claim support failures to the same downstream failure contract as request_failed, or the service must return an accepted:false request_failed result instead of throwing after rejectIncidentUpdate. Add route-level coverage proving first-attempt owner seam/canonical reader failures return 503, not 400, for POST/PATCH support incident paths.
evidence: Reviewed changed code files: apps/core/src/support/support-evidence.ts, apps/core/src/platform/operator-api.ts, apps/core/src/runtime/runtime-lifecycle.ts, packages/db/src/support.ts, packages/db/src/operator-auth.ts, packages/db/src/index.ts, and changed support/operator-auth tests. Ran node --experimental-strip-types --test apps/core/test/support/support-evidence.contract.test.ts apps/core/test/support/support-usage-audit.contract.test.ts apps/core/test/platform/operator-support.integration.test.ts packages/db/test/operator-auth-store.integration.test.ts packages/db/test/support-store.integration.test.ts: pass. Additional route probe with injected updateSupportIncident rejection returned HTTP 400 body {accepted:false,error:"support owner down"}, confirming operator-api.ts catch bypasses supportFailureStatus(request_failed).

notes: Independent r04 code review of e8c7c2d90755 found one blocking API status mapping defect. The replay/idempotency store path now records post-claim failures as rejected request_failed and DB/service tests cover owner seam and canonical reader replay behavior, and runtime auth evidence wiring now goes through createOperatorAuthStore().hasAllowedAuthEvidence(). However, the support HTTP POST/PATCH routes still catch thrown post-claim owner/canonical failures and return 400 before callers can observe the new request_failed status mapping; only a later replay maps request_failed to 503. This misclassifies downstream/runtime failures as client validation errors on the first attempt and makes the new API mapping incomplete.
